modern formulation philosophy hypothesis testing developed three between 1915-1933; Ronald Fisher (1890-1962), Jerzey Neyman (1884 -1981) Egon Pearson (1895-1980). Fisher developed principles statistical significance testing p-values, whilst Neyman Pearson took slightly different view formulated method today where compare competing hypotheses: alternative hypothesis (Ha), also developed notions Type errors.
know from undergraduate studies, with hypothesis testing using p-values outcomes possible statistical test result. Either test statistic result critical region, test result declared statistically significant (5%) significance level. this case logical choices possible researcher: they reject null hypothesis, accept possibility that Type error occurred Secondly, test statistic result outside critical region (the shaded areas below).
Here p-value greater than statistical significance level either there enough evidence reject there Type error. should note this same finding evidence favour lack evidence against hypothesis evidence which another common scientific error (see below). Another problem with that many researchers view accepting failure experiment. This view unfortunately rather poor science, accepting rejecting hypothesis positive result that contributes knowledge. Even refuted have learned something new, term ‘failure', should only applied errors experimental design, incorrect initial assumptions. That said, relative dearth reports negative outcome studies compared positive ones scientific literature should give some cause question here.
Neyman-Pearson approach attained status orthodoxy modern health science, should note that Fisher's inductive inference Neyman-Pearson's deductive approach actually philosophically opposed, many researchers unaware philosophical distinctions between them misinterpret this their reports.
Commonly researchers state type error rate p-value determined, power test statistic computed, (Neyman-Pearson's approach). then p-value mistakenly presented Type error rate probability that null hypothesis true (Fisher's approach), rather than correct interpretation that represents probability that random sampling would lead difference between sample means large, larger than that observed were true (Neyman-Pearson's approach). This seems subtle difference technically these very different things, Fisher viewed p-value objective measure evidence against null hypothesis, whilst Neyman Pearson not. They view that this (and particularly single test single study) never "proves" anything.
p-values represent conditional probabilities (conditional premise that true) example help understand this difference would that probability that given nurse female, Prob(female Nurse), around inverse probability, that given female nurse; Prob(Nurse| female), likely smaller than Arguments over this misinterpretation p-values continues lead widespread disagreement interpretation test results.
There also some good evidence that because p-values calculated they actually exaggerate evidence against null hypothesis (Berger Selke, 1987; Hubbard Lindsay, 2008) this represents most significant intended well just bit) criticisms p-values overall measure evidence.
result important not).
give some statistical evidence that phenomenon examining likely exists that about should very aware that ubiquitous p-value does provide objective, unambiguous measure evidence hypothesis testing. This argument been argued since 1930's with some researchers arguing that significance tests really represent modern sorcery rather than science (Bakan, 1966; Lambdin, 2012) that their counter-intuitive nature frequently leads confusion about terminology.
Logically also been noted that p-values fail meet simple logical condition required measure support, that hypothesis implies hypothesis converse should expect least much support there (Hubbard Lindsay, 2008; Schervish, 1996).
problem need practical methods that avoid both dismissing meaningful results, exaggerating evidence. Bayesian techniques have been suggestion replacement significance texting p-values, have take hold, probably because simplicity implementing p-values their widespread perceived objectivity (Thompson, 1998). Overall these concerns should emphasize importance repeated studies consideration findings larger context, ultimately this leads good argument value meta-analysis.
question remains, still relying p-values when there many issues with them, probably much better techniques?
Beat's maybe sooner become Bayesian's better science.
Bakan, (1966). test significance psychological research Psychological Bulletin, 423-437.

Hubbard, Lindsay, (2008). values useful measure evidence statistical significance testing Theory Psychology, 18(1), 69-88.
Lambdin, (2012). Significance tests sorcery: Science tests not. Theory Psychology, 22(67), 67-90.
